text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Criminal Record Expungement Assistance 11 Matching Service Providers GUIDANCE 2 GREATNESS BEYOND REENTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with criminal record and eviction expungement, job development, workplace training, financial literacy, felony rights, and reentry training. May be able to provide program participants with supplies such as personal protective equipment (PPE) and a desktop computer. Eligibility: Income must be at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: By appointment only (541) 385-6950 Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON DOUGLAS COUNTY OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Expungement: Individuals who have no criminal charges in the U.S. or foreign countries, no criminal convictions in the previous 3 years, no more than one conviction in the past 10 years, and are not currently on parole or probation. All other services: Must meet income guidelines. Prioritizes serving survivors of abuse or violent trauma and disabled individuals. Eligibility: Income below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level. Must live in or have a legal issue in Marion or Polk Counties. Hours: By appointment only (503) 581-5265 Visit Website More Details OREGON JUDICIAL BRANCH CRIMINAL RECORD EXPUNGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on how to file a petition to set aside or seal a conviction, an arrest, citation, charge, or a finding of Guilty Except Insane (GEI). Charges that may be set aside include most violations, misdemeanors, Class C felonies, and those felonies that may be treated as misdemeanors. Most Class A and Class B felony convictions may not be set aside. No type of traffic violation (such as speeding) or traffic crime (such as DUII) can be set aside. Violent felonies, most sex offenses and offenses involving child abuse may not be set aside. Allows some very old but serious drug convictions to be set aside. 1163 State Street Salem, OR 973012563 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 986-5500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH, RIGHTS & JUSTICE EXPUNGEMENT CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with expungement of juvenile criminal records. 1785 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 300 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Individuals with juvenile court convictions which were committed in the state of Oregon Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 232-2540 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON JUDICIAL BRANCH CRIMINAL RECORD EXPUNGEMENT YOUTH, RIGHTS & JUSTICE EXPUNGEMENT CLINIC
